gameStart.intro:
Perfect. Allow me to set the scene for you...

You have been tasked with escorting a carriage containing two crates of a precious cargo
to the coastal town of Solace. You're about three days into your journey, with a couple more to go.
As you begin to settle down for the night, you hear a slight rustling in a nearly bush. You stand betweenthe camp and the bush,
watching it warily, as an angry looking goblin jumps out at you, brandishing a rusty knife.

combatIntro2.intro:
The goblin goes down without much fight, but it makes you wonder why it might have
attacked you alone, and whether there are more lurking about.
As you hear a frantic rustling from behind you, you turn around just in time to
witness an abnormally large goblin making off with a crate of your precious cargo.
You try to chase it down, but are intercepted by another goblin,
this one also significantly larger than normal.

goblinChoice.prompt:
After taking out the goblin, you look around frantically, but to no avail.
The goblin that stole one of your crates is nowhere to be seen. If you continue to your
destination without it, you risk not being paid for your efforts, but following the
goblin may result in your untimely death.
goblinChoice.choice1:
You run off in the direction of the goblin. After several minutes of running,
you find a small camp of goblins. There are four of them in total, plus a
clearly injured orc, seemingly listening to the goblin you were chasing.
goblinChoice.choice2:
You continue on to your destination, the town of Solace. It's on the larger side,
as far as towns go, with a few highly regarded inns and taverns, as well as the
home of the prestigious craftsman Toralf.

You make it there after a couple days, and follow the address you were given to Toralf's home
and workshop. He welcomes your arrival, and is willing to forget the issue of the missing crate.
He pays you the agreed 80 gold coins for your efforts, and you leave him to his creations.

goblinCampChoice.prompt:
You don't stand much chance given the current circumstances, so it may be worth
backing off and staying alive. On the other hand, if you can manage to get the
drop on the orc somehow, you may be able to get this done yourself.
Quickly scouring the area, you notice a few dense trees near the camp,
which you may be able to use to sneak up on the orc.
goblinCampChoice.choice1:
You decide to play it safe, and make your way back to the carriage.
After another minute or two, you're continuing on your way to Solace.
goblinCampChoice.choice2:
You manage to sneak over to and climb one of the trees, waiting for the
perfect moment to strike. From up here, you notice the corpse of another
adventurer on the ground next to a large, rather bloody rock. It's safe 
to assume that brave soul is the cause of the orc's severe injuries.
Eventually, the orc shambles its way over to the tree, and
cautiously sits down next to it.
Now's your chance.
You drop down from the tree and cut off the orc's head before it even
has the chance to stand up. The goblins notice you, and run away
screaming in a language you don't understand.

townChoice.prompt:
As you walk back out into town, you start looking around for some more work. After a quick
glance around the town square, you notice a tavern, a store advertising various
magical and non-magical trinkets and gear, and a billboard showcasing a few different pieces of paper, one of which being a wanted poster.
townChoice.choice1:
As you walk into the {TAVERN NAME}, you notice a few things. The bar sits on the
opposite side of the room, with an energetic dwarven bartender enthusiastically
serving customers, a crowded table to your left is making a lot of noise,
but you can't quite see why, and there are a few other less crowded tables
of people eating food, drinking, or just chatting.
townChoice.choice2:
The {STORE NAME} is empty except for an exotic looking shopkeeper, with a colorful
and flashy outfit that has to have taken months to make.

'Gooood afternoon! What can I get for ya? You look like an adventurer of some kind,
am I right? I bet I'm right! Maybe one of these nifty little things will do the trick!'

He gestures towards a shelf holding a few different strange lookingitems, each presumably
with their own magical oddities. following his gaze, you see a sword sheath
of some kind, a small blue glowing stone, and a ring with some sort of symbol
on it, which you can't quite make out from here.

'Now, I haven't actually had the opportunity to test these myself, buuut I can at least tell you what they allegedly do! This here sheath is supposed to amplify the cutting abilities of and blade it holds, this strange rock supposedly increases your mobility, and this ring should allow you to turn invisible temporarily. How's that sound?'

With the gold you got from Toralf, you can only afford to buy one of these
items at most, but you get the feeling that they'll be of some help to you in the future.
townChoice.choice3:
The billboard contains a few different wanted posters, a calendar, and a letter
signed by the mayor, seemingly needing help with something. The wanted poster
shows a rough sketch of a notorious pirate captain, Luigi Vampa, with a massive
bounty of 3000 gold on his head. The poster reads: 

WANTED DEAD OR ALIVE
LUIGI VAMPA
FOR THE CRIMES OF GRAND LARCENY, MURDER, AND KIDNAPPING
BEWARE, HIM AND HIS CREW ARE VERY DANGEROUS
EXERCISE EXTREME CAUTION IF ENCOUNTERED
REWARD FOR PROOF OF DEATH: 3000 GOLD COINS
